questions.prompt: A user on a corporate full-tunnel VPN is no longer being filtered by DNSFilter. The agent service is running and was working before VPN connect. What's the most likely cause?
choices: The agent's Site key is wrong
Full-tunnel VPN routes DNS through the VPN's own resolver, bypassing the Roaming Client
The user's Allow list was deleted
The user is on a different organisation
questions.explanation: Full-tunnel VPNs send all traffic, including DNS, through the VPN's resolvers, so the local Roaming Client never sees the queries. The fix is split-tunnel, or pointing the VPN's DNS at 127.0.0.2 to route through the agent.

questions.prompt: DNSFilter's Roaming Client is supported on Windows Server, shared-desktop environments, and Windows 365 Cloud PC.
questions.explanation: DNSFilter explicitly does not support Windows Server, shared desktop environments, or Windows 365 Cloud PC. Supported is Windows 10/11 x64 or ARM64 (and macOS / mobile via their respective clients).
